reviewSummary.summary: The draft artifact is heavily polluted with upstream home_page controls, technical naming, duplicate ownership, and non-actionable decorative elements. Critical login workflow elements such as Username field, Password field, Login button, and Failed authentication message are missing, which blocks reliable downstream login automation generation.

issues.message: The Login page Username field required for authentication workflows is missing from the approved element set.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a semantically named Username field element using a stable locator such as the username input placeholder, form control name, or unique id.

issues.message: The Login page Password field required for authentication and password masking validation is missing from the approved element set.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a Password field element with a stable id/name locator suitable for both entry and masking validation.

issues.message: The Login button used to submit authentication is missing from the approved element set.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a semantically named Login button element with a stable locator.

issues.message: The workflow explicitly requires validation of the generic 'Failed' authentication message, but no validation/message element exists in the approved model.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a reusable failed_authentication_message element using a stable alert, toast, banner, or text locator.

issues.message: Customer signup button belongs to the upstream home_page workflow and is unrelated to the login_page workflow scope.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this element from the login_page artifact and reuse ownership from the upstream home_page resource.

issues.message: Clean survey button is unrelated to the login workflow and should not be owned by login_page.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this element from the login_page artifact and preserve ownership in the home_page resource.

issues.message: Site survey button is unrelated to the login workflow and should not be modeled as a login_page element.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this element from the login_page artifact and preserve ownership in the home_page resource.

issues.message: The Home button already exists as a shared upstream navigation control and is duplicated by a generic anchor element.
issues.suggested_fix: Keep only the semantic Home button element with locator id=auto_home_btn and remove the duplicate generic anchor.

issues.message: Locator xpath=//a is dangerously generic and likely to match multiple links across the application.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove this duplicate anchor element or replace it with a stable semantic locator tied to a specific control.

issues.message: Element naming exposes raw DOM ids instead of business-readable semantics.
Element naming is implementation-centric rather than workflow-centric.
Element naming exposes technical DOM identifiers and the control is not relevant to login workflow validation.
issues.suggested_fix: Either remove the element if not required for login workflows or rename semantically to notification_button.

issues.message: Element naming exposes raw implementation details instead of reusable navigation semantics.
Background container is decorative/layout-only and provides no meaningful automation value.
issues.suggested_fix: Remove the decorative background element from the approved model.

issues.message: The workflow requires authenticated-state validation after successful login, but no authenticated user identity display or authenticated-only feature area elements are modeled.
issues.suggested_fix: Add semantically named authenticated_state validation elements in the appropriate upstream page model if observable in the UI.

approved_elements.name: person_profile_button
approved_elements.description: Opens the Login page from home_page guest state.

approved_elements.name: back_button
approved_elements.description: Returns the user to home_page without authentication.

approved_elements.name: home_button
approved_elements.description: Navigates the user back to home_page.
